title: jansky-research: A CPU-only, reproducible toolkit for amateur radio-astronomy analyses
description:
A tested, CPU-only Python toolkit for amateur, reproducible radio-astronomy analyses, built on the jansky course library. It bundles five public-data research slices, each run on real public data, put through an adversarial science-review gate, and written up honestly as an AASTeX paper:

  • frbstats — FRB burst statistics (validates the CHIME repeater width result), automated with an Apache Airflow pipeline on rootless Podman;
  • frbperiod — recovers FRB 20180916B's 16.35-day activity period from the public CHIME/FRB catalogue;
  • hi — recovers the flat inner Milky Way rotation curve from LAB HI 21 cm data;
  • driftsearch — a reproducible SETI Doppler-drift injection-recovery benchmark, with an honest Voyager-1 null;
  • spectra — shows that raw TGSS×NVSS ultra-steep-spectrum selection is dominated by the TGSS flux-scale systematic (a cautionary negative).

Three validations and two negative/cautionary results; every reported number regenerates from the pipeline. Developed collaboratively with Anthropic's Claude; an AI assistant is not an eligible author and is credited in the acknowledgements only.
notes: Developed collaboratively with Anthropic's Claude (large language model). An AI assistant is not an eligible author; it is credited in the acknowledgements and as a software citation, not in the author list.
